feed.title: The RAM Index — Memory Market Intelligence
feed.subtitle: DRAM prices, semiconductor trade, and macroeconomic signals curated by The Ramification Index.

author.name: Khayyam Wakil / The ARC Institute of Knowware

entry.title: Lexar regional manager says that RAM prices are expected to double by the end of the year — 'discounts' and stabilized prices result from distributors getting rid of old stock or sourcing products from other regions
entry.summary: Don't get your hopes up.
entry.content:
Don't get your hopes up.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Mind the gap! The semiconductor industry is relying on the wrong materials
entry.summary: 2D materials are widely seen as a promising path toward better computer chips. Researchers at TU Wien have now shown that some of these materials are unsuitable due to an underestimated effect. But there are alternatives.
entry.content:
2D materials are widely seen as a promising path toward better computer chips. Researchers at TU Wien have now shown that some of these materials are unsuitable due to an underestimated effect. But there are alternatives.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Enthusiast builds his own RAM in garden shed cleanroom — fledgling array of memory cells groundwork for much larger future project | Tom's Hardware
entry.summary: One small step, but the good doctor asks us to ‘stay tuned’ for a PC scale result.
entry.content:
One small step, but the good doctor asks us to ‘stay tuned’ for a PC scale result.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: A Guide to Understanding GPUs and Maximizing GPU Utilization | Towards Data Science
entry.summary: In an age of constrained compute, learn how to optimize GPU efficiency through understanding architecture, bottlenecks, and fixes ranging from simple PyTorch commands to custom kernels.
entry.content:
In an age of constrained compute, learn how to optimize GPU efficiency through understanding architecture, bottlenecks, and fixes ranging from simple PyTorch commands to custom kernels.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Cerebras, an A.I. Chip Maker, Files to Go Public as Tech Offerings Ramp Up - The New York Times
entry.summary: The Silicon Valley chip maker filed a prospectus just as SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI prepare for their own listings, in what is shaping up to be a wave of enormous initial public offerings.
entry.content:
The Silicon Valley chip maker filed a prospectus just as SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI prepare for their own listings, in what is shaping up to be a wave of enormous initial public offerings.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project… | Laurie Kirk | 25 comments
entry.summary:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM.
But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s!
In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM!
By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency.
The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it.
Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn
entry.content:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM! By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency. The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it. Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: New Cambridge human brain-inspired chip could slash AI energy use — new type of memristor has roughly a million times lower switching current than conventional devices | Tom's Hardware
entry.summary: The devices use a self-assembled p-n junction inside the oxide film instead of conductive filaments.
entry.content:
The devices use a self-assembled p-n junction inside the oxide film instead of conductive filaments.

entry.title: Making Software: How is data stored?
entry.summary: Invariably, it involves trying to trap electrons in increasingly complicated ways.

entry.title: Inverse-designed nanophotonic neural network accelerators for ultra-compact optical computing | Nature Communications
entry.summary: Scaling of optical computing requires computationally dense and efficient hardware. Here, the authors inverse-design and experimentally demonstrate ultracompact nanophotonic neural network accelerators with high computational density, enabling scalable and energy-efficient analog photonic computing.
entry.content:
Scaling of optical computing requires computationally dense and efficient hardware. Here, the authors inverse-design and experimentally demonstrate ultracompact nanophotonic neural network accelerators with high computational density, enabling scalable and energy-efficient analog photonic computing.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: John Carmack muses using a long fiber line as an L2 cache for streaming AI data — programmer imagines fiber as alternative to DRAM
entry.summary: Yes, it's delay-line memory, but with lasers!
entry.content:
Yes, it's delay-line memory, but with lasers!

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Chinese scientists shrink semiconductor chip into fibre as thin as human hair
entry.summary: Advance allows fibres to compute like chips or display information like transistors, paving the way for machine-wearable smart textiles.
entry.content:
Advance allows fibres to compute like chips or display information like transistors, paving the way for machine-wearable smart textiles.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Adeia sues AMD for patent infringement over semiconductor technology | Reuters
entry.summary: Technology licensing company Adeia filed two lawsuits against Advanced Micro Devices in Texas federal court on Monday for allegedly infringing ten patents related to semiconductor innovations.
entry.content:
Technology licensing company Adeia filed two lawsuits against Advanced Micro Devices in Texas federal court on Monday for allegedly infringing ten patents related to semiconductor innovations.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: 3D NAND: The Most Scalable Semiconductor

entry.content:
16:12 - Typo, meant to say 2013 not 2023Links:- Patreon (Support the channel directly!): https://www.patreon.com/Asianometry- X: https://twitter.com/asianome...

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Scientists smash record in stacking semiconductor transistors for large-area electronics
entry.summary: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST; Saudi Arabia) researchers have set a record in microchip design, achieving the first six-stack hybrid CMOS (complementary metal-oxide semiconductor) for large-area electronics. With no other reported hybrid CMOS exceeding two stacks, the feat marks a new benchmark in integration density and efficiency, opening possibilities in electronic miniaturization and performance.
entry.content:
King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST; Saudi Arabia) researchers have set a record in microchip design, achieving the first six-stack hybrid CMOS (complementary metal-oxide semiconductor) for large-area electronics. With no other reported hybrid CMOS exceeding two stacks, the feat marks a new benchmark in integration density and efficiency, opening possibilities in electronic miniaturization and performance.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: What Does Semiconductor Disruption Look Like?
entry.summary: While everybody seems to agree that AI will disrupt semiconductor design and EDA tools, nobody has yet suggested what a disrupted flow would actually look like.
entry.content:
While everybody seems to agree that AI will disrupt semiconductor design and EDA tools, nobody has yet suggested what a disrupted flow would actually look like.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Female-founded semiconductor AI startup SixSense raises $8.5M | TechCrunch
entry.summary: SixSense offers an AI-powered platform that helps chip makers prevent defects. It landed backing led by Peak XV’s Surge (formerly Sequoia India & SEA).
entry.content:
SixSense offers an AI-powered platform that helps chip makers prevent defects. It landed backing led by Peak XV’s Surge (formerly Sequoia India & SEA).

entry.title: World’s first semiconductor made by quantum tech stuns chip industry
entry.summary: In a first, Australian scientists turned to quantum machine learning to model semiconductor design, outperforming classical approaches.
entry.content:
In a first, Australian scientists turned to quantum machine learning to model semiconductor design, outperforming classical approaches.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: New RAMBO attack steals data using RAM in air-gapped computers
entry.summary: A novel side-channel attack dubbed "RAMBO" (Radiation of Air-gapped Memory Bus for Offense) generates electromagnetic radiation from a device's RAM to send data from air-gapped computers.
entry.content:
A novel side-channel attack dubbed  "RAMBO" (Radiation of Air-gapped Memory Bus for Offense) generates electromagnetic radiation from a device's RAM to send data from air-gapped computers.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: World's 1st graphene semiconductor could power future quantum computers
entry.summary: Scientists overcame a limitation in graphene to harness the material as a working semiconductor at terahertz frequencies with 10 times the mobility of silicon.
entry.content:
Scientists overcame a limitation in graphene to harness the material as a working semiconductor at terahertz frequencies with 10 times the mobility of silicon.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Canadian government ups semiconductor focus, invests $36 million in Ranovus | BetaKit
entry.summary: Ottawa-based Ranovus Inc. has secured $36 million through the Canadian federal government’s Strategic Innovation Fund.
entry.content:
